title: Token-2022 createMint passes TOKEN_2022_PROGRAM_ID as the program ID argument

params.passDetail: createMint passes {expected} as its programId argument — Token-2022 features will work as designed.
params.failForbiddenDetail: createMint passes {got} (legacy Token program) where Token-2022 was intended. The mint will be owned by the legacy program; Token-2022 features (transfer hooks, transfer fees, confidential transfers) will not work and there is NO on-chain fix — a mint's owner program is part of its identity.
params.failMissingDetail: createMint omits its programId argument (or passes undefined), which silently defaults to the LEGACY Token program even though the file imports {expected}. Pass {expected} explicitly.
params.failOtherDetail: createMint passes '{got}' as programId; expected {expected}. If this is a custom constant, alias it to {expected} or pass the canonical name so the trap detector and downstream readers agree.
params.absentCallDetail: Handler is in the SPL-token scope but does not call createMint; the Token-2022 pin rule does not apply here.
params.scopeNotMetDetail: File does not import TOKEN_2022_PROGRAM_ID; legacy-token codebase — Token-2022 pin rule does not apply.

provenance.note: Trap #1 of the Tier-1 five. Closes via proof-as-classifier once the new vetted checker kind argEqualsConstantIdentifier is in the registry. The -synth suffix on the id keeps the staged rule distinct from the production-promoted version.
